open_gaps.SEC-002: LOW (unchanged). Still no numPages>1000 cap at the viewer.js loadDocument() load boundary, and no cumulative-queued-bytes cap at the multi-file ingest boundaries (merge.js queued PDFs, img2pdf.js queued images). New this tick: interleave.js is a FOURTH load boundary but the MILDEST — it reads only ONE fresh second PDF (replace-on-repick, NOT a queue), so its peak memory is bounded at base + <=50MB and it needs NO separate cumulative cap. All are client-side-only DoS (uploader's own tab) on the 1.6 GiB box. Single right developer fix remains: page-count cap once at viewer load (covers all per-page consumers) + cumulative-bytes caps in merge.js/img2pdf.js addFiles(). No server-side risk.

notes: 39 JS modules (+1 since 00:30 review: interleave.js, TASK-349 VERIFIED). interleave.js (zipper-merge open doc A with one second PDF B -> A1,B1,A2,B2... surplus appended; optional reverse-B toggle) is security CLEAN: full upload.js validation applied LOCALLY to the second file (extension + MIME + non-empty + 50MB cap + %PDF- magic-byte check) before pdf-lib parse; scoped to a SINGLE second file (replace-on-repick, not a cumulative queue) so peak mem bounded at base+<=50MB; filename sanitized [^a-zA-Z0-9._-]->_ slice(180) -> <base>_interleaved.pdf; status/info via textContent only (no innerHTML with the user filename); safe Blob download + URL.revokeObjectURL; PDFLib-unready/no-doc/no-second guards; errors caught + surfaced via inline status + EventBus.ERROR; open viewer doc never mutated (base bytes from doc.getData()). Folded into SEC-002 as the mildest member (no separate cap needed). Full-JS audit across all 39 modules: every innerHTML hit is either a COMMENT or `el.innerHTML=''` (clearing a container — no user/PDF data ever interpolated); zero eval/Function/document.write/outerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML; zero fetch/XHR/importScripts/remote-src (100% client-side, nothing leaves the device); no hardcoded secrets (grep hits are page-range parser 'token' vars + password-protect UI labels). upload.js still enforces ext+MIME+empty+size(50MB)+%PDF- magic with sanitized filenames.
